content: Is there a wrapping function to allocate pages from buddy system, instead of calling kernel::bindings::get_free_pages?

I am doing a research project for container memory mangement. I'd like to maintain and manage a set of pages for each container entity. I think allocating these pages directly from buddy system is better than allocating from kmalloc or vmalloc.
By the way, does Rust currently have support for set data structures such as HashSet?
There are support for a few different data structures, but not hash set.
Does the current rbtree and xarray not meet your needs?
Thanks a lot. I found https://rust.docs.kernel.org/kernel/page/struct.Page.html is all my need.
While both rbtree and xarray could work, I would prefer HashSet since I don't require sorting functionality.
